similarity_name: MS/MS spectral cosine or learned spectral similarity
definition: Compare tandem mass spectra using peak-list cosine, modified cosine, Spec2Vec, or another spectral similarity score. This audits whether predictions or annotations rely on evaluation spectra that are close to known training/library spectra.
example_usage: python spectrum_cosine_similarity.py --train train_spectra.csv --eval eval_spectra.csv --out pairwise_similarity.csv --peaks-col peaks --tolerance 0.01

citations.title: Spec2Vec: Improved mass spectral similarity scoring through learning of structural relationships

evidence.summary: The paper states that spectral similarity is central to library matching and molecular networking.
It compares spectral similarity scores against structural similarity and library-matching performance.
It highlights computational and structural-analogue retrieval advantages of learned spectral embeddings.

limitations: Raw cosine is sensitive to peak filtering, mass tolerance, intensity normalization, adducts, and instrument conditions.
Modified cosine and Spec2Vec may be more appropriate for structurally related but non-identical molecules.
Spectral similarity is not identical to molecular structural similarity.
quality_gates: Report preprocessing, peak filtering, tolerance, intensity transform, precursor handling, and ion mode.
Compare raw cosine with modified cosine or learned spectral embeddings when annotations permit.
Check whether instrument, collision energy, or batch effects dominate spectral similarity.
